class Concept01FullPipelineDependencies(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Technical background using a custom grid to avoid NumberPlane issues
        grid = VGroup()
        for x in range(-7, 8):
            grid.add(Line(start=[x, -4, 0], end=[x, 4, 0], stroke_width=0.5, stroke_opacity=0.2, color=GRAY))
        for y in range(-4, 5):
            grid.add(Line(start=[-7, y, 0], end=[7, y, 0], stroke_width=0.5, stroke_opacity=0.2, color=GRAY))
        self.add(grid)

        # Formula at the top as requested
        formula = MathTex(
            r"\text{Pipeline} = \{ \text{Data} \to \text{Preprocessing} \to \text{Model} \to \text{HPO} \to \text{Deployment} \}",
            font_size=28
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        self.add(formula)

        # Create the four main circles
        step_names = ["Data\nRetrieval", "Preprocessing", "Model\nSearch", "Deployment"]
        # Space them out across the screen
        positions = [LEFT * 4.5, LEFT * 1.5, RIGHT * 1.5, RIGHT * 4.5]
        
        circles = VGroup()
        labels = VGroup()
        
        for name, pos in zip(step_names, positions):
            circle = Circle(radius=1.1, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.15)
            circle.move_to(pos)
            label = Text(name, font_size=20).move_to(pos)
            circles.add(circle)
            labels.add(label)

        # Connect with thick arrows
        arrows = VGroup()
        for i in range(len(circles) - 1):
            arrow = Arrow(
                start=circles[i].get_right(),
                end=circles[i+1].get_left(),
                buff=0.1,
                stroke_width=10,
                color=BLUE,
                max_tip_length_to_length_ratio=0.15
            )
            arrows.add(arrow)

        self.add(circles, labels, arrows)

        # Animation sequence
        self.wait(1)

        # Pulse animation on Data Retrieval
        # Voice: "Imagine your raw data as the foundation; a change here..."
        self.play(
            Indicate(circles[0], color=GOLD, scale_factor=1.2),
            run_time=2
        )

        # Propagation animation
        # Voice: "...ripples through the entire structure, dictating which models are viable..."
        for i in range(len(arrows)):
            self.play(
                arrows[i].animate.set_color(GOLD),
                circles[i+1].animate.set_color(GOLD),
                run_time=0.7,
                rate_func=smooth
            )

        self.wait(3)

from manim import *

class Concept01Scene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Background setup - manual grid for technical feel as NumberPlane is restricted
        grid = VGroup()
        for x in range(-7, 8):
            grid.add(Line([x, -4, 0], [x, 4, 0], stroke_opacity=0.1, stroke_width=1))
        for y in range(-4, 5):
            grid.add(Line([-7, y, 0], [7, y, 0], stroke_opacity=0.1, stroke_width=1))
        self.add(grid)

        # Formula at the top
        formula = MathTex(
            r"Pipeline = \{Data \to Preprocessing \to Model \to HPO \to Deployment\}",
            font_size=32
        ).to_edge(UP)

        # Nodes: Data Retrieval, Preprocessing, Model Search, Deployment
        titles = ["Data\nRetrieval", "Pre-\nprocessing", "Model\nSearch", "Deployment"]
        colors = [BLUE, RED, GREEN, YELLOW]
        positions = [LEFT * 4.5, LEFT * 1.5, RIGHT * 1.5, RIGHT * 4.5]

        nodes = VGroup()
        for t, c, p in zip(titles, colors, positions):
            circ = Circle(radius=0.9, color=c, fill_opacity=0.1)
            circ.move_to(p)
            lbl = Text(t, font_size=18).move_to(p)
            nodes.add(VGroup(circ, lbl))

        # Thick Arrows connecting the nodes
        arrows = VGroup()
        for i in range(len(nodes) - 1):
            arr = Arrow(
                nodes[i][0].get_right(), 
                nodes[i+1][0].get_left(), 
                buff=0.1, 
                stroke_width=10, 
                max_tip_length_to_length_ratio=0.2,
                color=WHITE
            )
            arrows.add(arr)

        # Construction sequence
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.play(
            LaggedStart(*[Create(n) for n in nodes], lag_ratio=0.2),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.play(
            LaggedStart(*[Create(a) for a in arrows], lag_ratio=0.2),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Ripple animation: Pulse Data Retrieval then propagate color change
        # Pulse animation
        self.play(Indicate(nodes[0], scale_factor=1.2, color=WHITE))

        # Propagate color change from blue (start) to gold through arrows and nodes
        propagation_anims = []
        for i in range(len(arrows)):
            # Change arrow to gold
            propagation_anims.append(arrows[i].animate.set_color(GOLD))
            # Change subsequent node circle to gold
            propagation_anims.append(nodes[i+1][0].animate.set_color(GOLD))

        self.play(
            LaggedStart(*propagation_anims, lag_ratio=0.4),
            run_time=3
        )
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class RetrievalAugmentedPlanning(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Create icons
        agent_manager = Text("Agent Manager").to_edge(UP)
        knowledge_base = Text("Knowledge Base (Cloud)").next_to(agent_manager, LEFT).shift(LEFT)
        plan_a = Text("Plan A (Transformer)").next_to(agent_manager, RIGHT).shift(RIGHT)
        plan_b = Text("Plan B (CNN)").next_to(agent_manager, RIGHT).shift(RIGHT * 2)
        plan_c = Text("Plan C (LSTM)").next_to(agent_manager, RIGHT).shift(RIGHT * 3)

        # Connect Knowledge Base to Agent Manager
        arrow_kb_to_manager = Arrow(start=knowledge_base.get_bottom(), end=agent_manager.get_top())
        self.play(FadeIn(knowledge_base), FadeIn(agent_manager))
        self.play(Create(arrow_kb_to_manager))

        # Branch out into multiple plans
        self.wait()
        plans = VGroup(plan_a, plan_b, plan_c)
        plans.arrange(RIGHT, buff=2).next_to(agent_manager, DOWN)
        self.play(Create(plans))

        # Explain how plans are generated using RAP
        rap_text = MathTex(r"\mathbf{P} = \mathcal{A}_{mgr}(\mathrm{RAP}(R))").to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN)
        self.play(Create(rap_text), Write(rap_text))

class Concept03PseudoExecutionHandoff(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Top half: Data Agent
        data_agent = Text("Data Agent").to_edge(UP)
        data_block = Rectangle(width=3, height=1).next_to(data_agent, DOWN)

        # Bottom half: Model Agent
        model_agent = Text("Model Agent").to_edge(DOWN)
        model_task_block = Rectangle(width=3, height=1).next_to(model_agent, UP)

        # Arrow and insights

        data_insights = Text("O_d").move_to(arrow.get_center())

        self.wait(0.5)

        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(Transform(search_efficiency, Text("Search Efficiency: 90%").to_edge(RIGHT)), run_time=1)
        self.wait(1.5)
        self.play(FadeOut(data_insights), FadeOut(arrow))

        # Equation
        equation = MathTex(r"s_i^m = \mathrm{PD}(R, \mathcal{A}_m, \mathbf{p}_i, O_i^d)").to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(FadeIn(equation))
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import math

class InverseMapping(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Diagram Section
        input_rect = Rectangle(width=4.5, height=1.5, color=BLUE)
        input_title = Text("Desired Behavior", font_size=24).move_to(input_rect.get_center() + UP * 0.3)
        input_sub = Text("(Rise Time, SS Error, Stability)", font_size=18).move_to(input_rect.get_center() + DOWN * 0.3)
        input_group = VGroup(input_rect, input_title, input_sub).shift(UP * 2 + LEFT * 3)

        output_rect = Rectangle(width=3.5, height=1.5, color=GREEN)
        output_title = Text("PID Constants", font_size=24).move_to(output_rect.get_center())
        output_group = VGroup(output_rect, output_title).shift(UP * 2 + RIGHT * 3)

        arrow = Line(input_group.get_right(), output_group.get_left(), color=YELLOW).add_tip()
        
        diagram = VGroup(input_group, output_group, arrow)

        # 2. Formula Section
        formula = MathTex(r"U = K_p e + K_i \int e dt + K_d \frac{de}{dt}", font_size=36).shift(UP * 0.5)

        # 3. Plot Section (Manual construction to avoid restricted identifiers)
        # Origin for the plot
        origin_point = DOWN * 3 + LEFT * 4
        x_axis = Line(origin_point, origin_point + RIGHT * 8, color=WHITE)
        y_axis = Line(origin_point, origin_point + UP * 2.5, color=WHITE)
        x_label = Text("Time", font_size=16).next_to(x_axis, DOWN)
        y_label = Text("RPM", font_size=16).next_to(y_axis, LEFT)

        # Dashed line (manual simulation using small lines)
        setpoint_y = origin_point[1] + 1.5
        setpoint_line = VGroup(*[
            Line(
                [origin_point[0] + i * 0.2, setpoint_y, 0], 
                [origin_point[0] + i * 0.2 + 0.1, setpoint_y, 0],
                color=GRAY
            ) for i in range(40)
        ])
        setpoint_text = Text("Setpoint", font_size=14).move_to([origin_point[0] + 7.5, setpoint_y + 0.2, 0])

        # Curve: Damped oscillation manually calculated
        curve = VMobject(color=RED)
        points = []
        for i in range(101):
            t_val = i / 100.0 * 7.0  # t from 0 to 7
            # Formula: 1.5 * (1 - exp(-0.7*t) * cos(3*t))
            val = 1.5 * (1.0 - math.exp(-0.7 * t_val) * math.cos(3.0 * t_val))
            points.append([origin_point[0] + t_val, origin_point[1] + val, 0])
        curve.set_points_as_corners(points)

        # Metrics indicators (simulating braces with lines)
        # Rise time (first crossing of 1.5)
        rise_x = 0.5
        rise_line = Line([origin_point[0], origin_point[1] - 0.2, 0], [origin_point[0] + rise_x, origin_point[1] - 0.2, 0], color=BLUE)
        rise_tick1 = Line([origin_point[0], origin_point[1] - 0.1, 0], [origin_point[0], origin_point[1] - 0.3, 0], color=BLUE)
        rise_tick2 = Line([origin_point[0] + rise_x, origin_point[1] - 0.1, 0], [origin_point[0] + rise_x, origin_point[1] - 0.3, 0], color=BLUE)
        rise_label = Text("Rise Time", font_size=16, color=BLUE).next_to(rise_line, DOWN, buff=0.1)
        rise_group = VGroup(rise_line, rise_tick1, rise_tick2, rise_label)

        # Steady State Error (difference at the end)
        error_x = origin_point[0] + 7.0
        error_line = Line([error_x + 0.2, origin_point[1] + 1.5, 0], [error_x + 0.2, points[-1][1], 0], color=GREEN)
        error_tick1 = Line([error_x + 0.1, origin_point[1] + 1.5, 0], [error_x + 0.3, origin_point[1] + 1.5, 0], color=GREEN)
        error_tick2 = Line([error_x + 0.1, points[-1][1], 0], [error_x + 0.3, points[-1][1], 0], color=GREEN)
        error_label = Text("SS Error", font_size=16, color=GREEN).next_to(error_line, RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        error_group = VGroup(error_line, error_tick1, error_tick2, error_label)

        # --- Animations ---
        self.play(FadeIn(input_group), FadeIn(output_group), Create(arrow))
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # "Flips the script"
        self.play(diagram.animate.rotate(PI))
        self.wait(1)

        # Plot construction
        self.play(Create(x_axis), Create(y_axis), Write(x_label), Write(y_label))
        self.play(Create(setpoint_line), FadeIn(setpoint_text))
        self.play(Create(curve), run_time=2)
        self.play(FadeIn(rise_group))
        self.play(FadeIn(error_group))
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import math

class Concept01InverseMapping(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Use basic color constants to avoid undefined identifier errors
        c_blue = BLUE
        c_red = RED
        c_white = WHITE
        c_green = GREEN
        c_yellow = YELLOW
        c_gray = GRAY
        
        # --- Top Section: Diagram ---
        # Traditional Flow: PID Constants -> Motor Behavior
        box_pid = Rectangle(width=4.0, height=1.5, color=c_red).shift(UP * 2.2 + LEFT * 3)
        text_pid = VGroup(
            Text("PID Constants").scale(0.5),
            Text("Kp, Ki, Kd").scale(0.3)
        ).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.1).move_to(box_pid)

        box_behavior = Rectangle(width=4.0, height=1.5, color=c_blue).shift(UP * 2.2 + RIGHT * 3)
        text_behavior = VGroup(
            Text("Desired Behavior").scale(0.5),
            Text("Rise Time, SSE, Stability").scale(0.3)
        ).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.1).move_to(box_behavior)

        conn_arrow = Arrow(box_pid.get_right(), box_behavior.get_left(), color=c_white)
        formula = MathTex(r"U = K_p e + K_i \int e dt + K_d \frac{de}{dt}").scale(0.6).next_to(box_behavior, DOWN)
        
        entire_diagram = VGroup(box_pid, text_pid, box_behavior, text_behavior, conn_arrow, formula)

        # Initial Animation
        self.play(FadeIn(box_pid), FadeIn(text_pid), FadeIn(box_behavior), FadeIn(text_behavior), Create(conn_arrow))
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # "Flips the script" - Rotate the entire diagram 180 degrees
        # Using [0,0,1] for axis to avoid 'OUT' which was flagged as undefined
        self.play(entire_diagram.animate.rotate(3.14159, axis=[0, 0, 1]), run_time=1.5)
        # Flip the text back so it is readable
        self.play(
            text_pid.animate.rotate(3.14159),
            text_behavior.animate.rotate(3.14159),
            formula.animate.rotate(3.14159),
            run_time=0.5
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # --- Bottom Section: Motor Velocity Plot ---
        # Build axes manually to avoid 'Axes' which was flagged as disallowed
        origin_pt = [-4, -2.5, 0]
        x_axis = Line(origin_pt, [-4 + 8, -2.5, 0], color=c_white)
        y_axis = Line(origin_pt, [-4, -2.5 + 3, 0], color=c_white)
        label_x = Text("Time").scale(0.4).next_to(x_axis, DOWN)
        label_y = Text("RPM").scale(0.4).next_to(y_axis, LEFT)
        
        self.play(Create(x_axis), Create(y_axis), Write(label_x), Write(label_y))

        # Manual dashed line for the setpoint at y = 1.0 (relative to origin_pt)
        # origin_pt[1] is -2.5, so setpoint is at -1.5
        setpoint_dashes = VGroup()
        for i_dash in range(25):
            x_start = -4 + (i_dash * 0.32)
            setpoint_dashes.add(Line([x_start, -1.5, 0], [x_start + 0.16, -1.5, 0], color=c_yellow))
        
        self.play(Create(setpoint_dashes))

        # Plot motor curve using VMobject and manual points to avoid 'FunctionGraph'
        # Curve represents a damped oscillation
        curve_points = []
        for i_step in range(101):
            v_t = i_step * 0.075 # range from 0 to 7.5
            # convergent value of 0.9 to show Steady State Error (setpoint is 1.0)
            v_y = 0.9 * (1.0 - math.exp(-0.6 * v_t) * math.cos(3.0 * v_t))
            curve_points.append([-4 + v_t, -2.5 + v_y, 0])
        
        motor_curve = VMobject(color=c_green)
        motor_curve.set_points_as_corners(curve_points)
        
        self.play(Create(motor_curve), run_time=3)

        # --- Indicators ---
        # Rise Time indicator (Manual construction instead of 'Brace')
        rt_width = 1.3
        rt_measure_line = Line([-4, -2.8, 0], [-4 + rt_width, -2.8, 0], color=c_blue)
        rt_cap_left = Line([-4, -2.9, 0], [-4, -2.7, 0], color=c_blue)
        rt_cap_right = Line([-4 + rt_width, -2.9, 0], [-4 + rt_width, -2.7, 0], color=c_blue)
        rt_text = Text("Rise Time").scale(0.3).next_to(rt_measure_line, DOWN, buff=0.1)

        # Steady State Error indicator (arrow showing the gap at the end)
        sse_arrow = Arrow([-4 + 7, -1.5, 0], [-4 + 7, -1.59, 0], color=c_red, buff=0, tip_length=0.15)
        sse_text = Text("Steady State Error").scale(0.3).next_to(sse_arrow, RIGHT, buff=0.1)

        self.play(
            Create(rt_measure_line), 
            Create(rt_cap_left), 
            Create(rt_cap_right), 
            Write(rt_text)
        )
        self.play(Create(sse_arrow), Write(sse_text))

        self.wait(2)

        kp_text = MathTex("K_p")
        ki_text = MathTex("K_i")
        kd_text = MathTex("K_d")
        output_items = VGroup(kp_text, ki_text, kd_text).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.2).move_to(output_box.get_center())
        output_group = VGroup(output_box, output_label, output_items).shift(RIGHT * 3.5 + UP * 1.5)

        # 2. Arrow connecting them
        arrow = Arrow(input_box.get_right(), output_box.get_left(), color=WHITE)
        diagram = VGroup(input_group, output_group, arrow)

        self.play(Create(input_group), Create(output_group), GrowArrow(arrow))
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. "Flip the script" - Rotate using animate.rotate to avoid Rotate class
        self.play(diagram.animate.rotate(PI), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Formula reference
        formula = MathTex(r"U = K_p e + K_i \int e dt + K_d \frac{de}{dt}", font_size=24)
        formula.to_corner(UL)
        self.play(Write(formula))

        # 6. Motor Velocity Plot using manual Lines and VMobject
        # Origin for plot
        origin = DOWN * 2.5 + LEFT * 4
        x_axis = Line(origin, origin + RIGHT * 8, color=WHITE)
        y_axis = Line(origin, origin + UP * 3, color=WHITE)
        
        x_label = Text("Time", font_size=16).next_to(x_axis, RIGHT)
        y_label = Text("RPM", font_size=16).next_to(y_axis, UP)
        
        # Dashed line manual implementation
        setpoint_val = 2.0
        setpoint_y = origin + UP * setpoint_val
        dashes = VGroup()
        for i in range(20):
            d_start = setpoint_y + RIGHT * (i * 0.4)
            d_end = setpoint_y + RIGHT * (i * 0.4 + 0.2)
            dashes.add(Line(d_start, d_end, color=GRAY, stroke_width=2))

        # Motor behavior curve manual implementation

        target_val = 1.8 # Lower than setpoint to show steady state error

            val = target_val * (1 - np.exp(-0.6 * t_step) * np.cos(2.5 * t_step))
            curve_points.append(origin + RIGHT * t_step + UP * val)
        
        motor_plot = VMobject(color=YELLOW).set_points_as_corners(curve_points)

        self.play(Create(x_axis), Create(y_axis), Write(x_label), Write(y_label))
        self.play(Create(dashes))
        self.play(Create(motor_plot), run_time=2)

        rt_start = origin + DOWN * 0.2
        rt_end = origin + RIGHT * 0.7 + DOWN * 0.2
        rt_line = Line(rt_start, rt_end, color=BLUE)
        rt_tick1 = Line(rt_start + UP*0.1, rt_start + DOWN*0.1, color=BLUE)
        rt_tick2 = Line(rt_end + UP*0.1, rt_end + DOWN*0.1, color=BLUE)
        rt_tag = Text("Rise Time", font_size=14, color=BLUE).next_to(rt_line, DOWN, buff=0.1)
        rise_time_meas = VGroup(rt_line, rt_tick1, rt_tick2, rt_tag)

        # Steady State Error (vertical)
        sse_x = origin + RIGHT * 7.5
        sse_bottom = sse_x + UP * target_val
        sse_top = sse_x + UP * setpoint_val
        sse_line = Line(sse_bottom, sse_top, color=RED)
        sse_tick1 = Line(sse_bottom + LEFT*0.1, sse_bottom + RIGHT*0.1, color=RED)
        sse_tick2 = Line(sse_top + LEFT*0.1, sse_top + RIGHT*0.1, color=RED)
        sse_tag = Text("Steady State Error", font_size=12, color=RED).next_to(sse_line, RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        sse_meas = VGroup(sse_line, sse_tick1, sse_tick2, sse_tag)

        self.play(Create(rise_time_meas))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(Create(sse_meas))

        self.wait(2)

        horiz_label = Text("Time", font_size=20).next_to(horiz_axis, DOWN)

        self.play(Create(horiz_axis), Create(vert_axis))
        self.play(Write(horiz_label), Write(vert_label))

        # 2. Velocity Plot (Fluctuating around 30 RPM)
        # Generate points manually to avoid 'x' variable issues in lambda
        t_vals = np.linspace(0, 5, 100)
        curve_points = []
        for t_step in t_vals:
            # Base height 2.5 (representing ~30 RPM) + fluctuations
            y_fluctuation = 2.5 + 0.3 * np.sin(t_step * 5) + 0.2 * np.cos(t_step * 11)
            curve_points.append(graph_origin + RIGHT * t_step + UP * y_fluctuation)
        
        velocity_curve = VMobject(color=YELLOW)
        velocity_curve.set_points_as_corners(curve_points)
        
        plot_title = Text("Motor Behavior", font_size=24).next_to(vert_axis, UP)
        self.play(Create(velocity_curve), Write(plot_title))
        self.wait(0.5)

        highlight_rect = Rectangle(

        self.play(FadeIn(highlight_rect), Write(highlight_text))
        self.wait()

        # 4. Histogram on the side
        hist_origin = RIGHT * 2.5 + DOWN * 1.5
        hist_h_line = Line(hist_origin, hist_origin + RIGHT * 4, color=WHITE)
        
        # Defining bar heights: the middle one is the mode (tallest)
        bar_heights = [0.7, 1.2, 3.2, 1.5, 0.8] 
        bars = VGroup()
        for i, h in enumerate(bar_heights):
            bar = Rectangle(
                width=0.5, 
                height=h,
                fill_color=YELLOW if i == 2 else BLUE,
                fill_opacity=0.7
            ).move_to(hist_origin + RIGHT * (0.6 + i * 0.7) + UP * (h / 2))
            bars.add(bar)
        
        hist_title = Text("Histogram", font_size=24).next_to(bars, UP)
        mode_label = Text("30 RPM", font_size=16).next_to(bars[2], DOWN)
        
        self.play(Create(hist_h_line))
        self.play(Create(bars), Write(hist_title), FadeIn(mode_label))
        self.wait()

        # 5. Stability Formula
        stability_formula = MathTex(
            r"\text{Stability} = \frac{\text{counts at mode}}{\text{counts not at mode}}",
            font_size=32
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.5)
        
        # Background for formula visibility
        formula_bg = SurroundingRectangle(stability_formula, color=BLACK, fill_opacity=0.8, stroke_width=0)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(formula_bg), Write(stability_formula))
        
        # Simple animation highlighting parts of the fraction
        num_box = SurroundingRectangle(bars[2], color=YELLOW)
        den_bars = VGroup(*[bars[j] for j in [0, 1, 3, 4]])
        den_box = SurroundingRectangle(den_bars, color=BLUE)
        
        self.play(Create(num_box))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(num_box, den_box))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(FadeOut(den_box))
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class Concept02QuantifyingStability(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Manual Coordinate System (Since Axes/NumberPlane are restricted)
        origin = LEFT * 5 + DOWN * 2
        x_len = 6
        y_len = 4
        
        x_axis = Line(origin, origin + RIGHT * x_len, color=WHITE)
        y_axis = Line(origin, origin + UP * y_len, color=WHITE)
        
        x_label = Text("Time", font_size=20).next_to(x_axis, DOWN)
        y_label = Text("RPM", font_size=20).next_to(y_axis, LEFT)
        
        # Grid lines/Ticks for reference
        y_30_pos = origin + UP * (y_len * 0.5) # 30 is midpoint between 20 and 40
        tick_30 = Line(y_30_pos + LEFT * 0.1, y_30_pos + RIGHT * 0.1)
        label_30 = Text("30", font_size=16).next_to(tick_30, LEFT)

        self.add(x_axis, y_axis, x_label, y_label, tick_30, label_30)

        # 2. Fluctuating Velocity Line
        # Mapping: X[0,10] -> [0, 6], Y[20, 40] -> [0, 4]
        def get_pos(t, rpm):
            return origin + RIGHT * (t * x_len / 10) + UP * ((rpm - 20) * y_len / 20)

        points = []
        for t in np.linspace(0, 10, 100):
            rpm = 30 + 0.8 * np.sin(t * 5) + 0.4 * np.cos(t * 12)
            points.append(get_pos(t, rpm))
        
        velocity_curve = VMobject(color=YELLOW)
        velocity_curve.set_points_as_corners(points)
        
        self.play(Create(velocity_curve), run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 3. Highlight last 20% (Time 8 to 10)
        p_start = get_pos(8, 20)
        p_end = get_pos(10, 40)
        
        highlight = Rectangle(
            width=p_end[0] - p_start[0],
            height=y_len,
            fill_color=BLUE,
            fill_opacity=0.3,
            stroke_width=0
        ).move_to(origin + RIGHT * (x_len * 0.9) + UP * (y_len / 2))
        
        sse_text = Text("Steady State Region", font_size=16, color=BLUE).next_to(highlight, UP)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(highlight), Write(sse_text))
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. Histogram on the right side
        hist_center = RIGHT * 3.5 + DOWN * 1
        bar_values = [0.5, 1.2, 3.5, 1.0, 0.4]
        bars = VGroup()
        for i, val in enumerate(bar_values):
            b = Rectangle(

                height=val,

                fill_color=GREEN if i == 2 else WHITE,
                stroke_width=1
            )
            bars.add(b)
        
        bars.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.1, aligned_edge=DOWN).move_to(hist_center, aligned_edge=DOWN)
        hist_title = Text("Histogram", font_size=20).next_to(bars, UP, buff=0.5)

class QuantifyingStability(Scene):

        # 1. Setup Axes for Velocity Plot
        # "Show a velocity plot where the motor is fluctuating around 30 RPM"
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 10, 2],
            y_range=[25, 35, 5],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=4,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False, "include_tip": False}
        ).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5).shift(UP * 0.5)
        
        x_label = MathTex("t").next_to(axes.x_axis, RIGHT)
        y_label = MathTex("v (RPM)").next_to(axes.y_axis, UP)
        
        # Velocity Data - Fluctuating around 30 RPM
        # Creating a specific set of data to ensure mode is 30
        velocities = [30, 31, 29, 30, 32, 28, 30, 31, 30, 30, 29, 30, 30, 30, 31, 29, 30, 30, 30, 30]
        times = np.linspace(0, 10, len(velocities))
        
        v_graph_data = axes.plot_line_graph(
            x_values=times, 
            y_values=velocities, 
            add_vertex_dots=True, 
            vertex_dot_style={"radius": 0.04, "color": YELLOW},
            line_color=YELLOW
        )

        # 2. Highlight Last 20% (x = 8 to 10)
        # "Highlight the last 20% of the x-axis with a different background color (e.g., light blue)"
        rect_width = axes.x_axis.get_unit_size() * 2
        rect_height = axes.y_axis.get_length()
        highlight_rect = Rectangle(
            width=rect_width, 
            height=rect_height,
            fill_color=BLUE_A, 
            fill_opacity=0.3, 
            stroke_width=0
        ).move_to(axes.c2p(9, 30))
        
        highlight_text = Text("Final 20%", font_size=18, color=BLUE_A).next_to(highlight_rect, UP, buff=0.1)

        # 3. Histogram on the Side
        # "Show a 'Histogram' appearing on the side, where the 'bin' at 30 RPM is significantly taller than the others"
        counts = Counter(velocities)
        bins = [28, 29, 30, 31, 32]
        
        hist_axes = Axes(
            x_range=[27, 33, 1],
            y_range=[0, 15, 5],
            x_length=4,
            y_length=3,
            axis_config={"include_tip": False, "include_numbers": False}
        ).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=0.5).shift(UP * 1.5)
        
        hist_label = Text("Histogram", font_size=24).next_to(hist_axes, UP)
        
        bars = VGroup()
        for b in bins:
            count = counts.get(b, 0)
            # Scale height according to hist_axes y range
            bar_height = count * (hist_axes.y_axis.get_length() / 15)
            bar = Rectangle(
                width=0.5,

                fill_opacity=0.8,

            ).move_to(hist_axes.c2p(b, 0), DOWN)

        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=1)

        self.wait(1)

        self.play(Write(formula))

from manim import *

class RewardDynamics(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup 3x3 Grid
        # Initial state: center is RED, top neighbor is BLUE, others RED
        colors = [
            RED, BLUE, RED,
            RED, RED,  RED,
            RED, RED,  RED
        ]
        grid = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=1).set_fill(colors[i], opacity=0.8).set_stroke(GRAY, 1)
            for i in range(9)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(rows=3, cols=3, buff=0)
        grid.shift(LEFT * 3)

        center_pixel = grid[4]
        # Neighbors: top(1), left(3), right(5), bottom(7)
        neighbors = VGroup(grid[1], grid[3], grid[5], grid[7])

        # 2. Manual Bar Chart using Axes and Rectangles
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 4, 1],
            y_range=[0, 10, 2],
            x_length=4,
            y_length=3,
            axis_config={"include_tip": False}
        ).shift(RIGHT * 3.5 + DOWN * 0.5)
        
        chart_label = Text("Cumulative Reward", font_size=24).next_to(axes, UP)
        
        # Bars
        bar1 = Rectangle(width=0.5, height=0.5, fill_opacity=1, color=BLUE).move_to(axes.c2p(1, 0), BOTTOM)
        bar2 = Rectangle(width=0.5, height=1.0, fill_opacity=1, color=BLUE).move_to(axes.c2p(2, 0), BOTTOM)
        bar3 = Rectangle(width=0.5, height=1.0, fill_opacity=1, color=BLUE).move_to(axes.c2p(3, 0), BOTTOM)
        bars = VGroup(bar1, bar2, bar3)

        # 3. Formula
        formula = MathTex(r"R_t = \text{Border}_{t} - \text{Border}_{t-1}").to_edge(UP)

        self.add(grid, axes, chart_label, formula, bars)
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. Highlight target and neighbors
        focus_rect = SurroundingRectangle(center_pixel, color=YELLOW, buff=0)
        neighbor_highlights = VGroup(*[
            SurroundingRectangle(n, color=WHITE, buff=-0.05).set_stroke(width=2) 
            for n in neighbors
        ])
        
        self.play(Create(focus_rect))
        self.play(FadeIn(neighbor_highlights))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Animate Flip and Rewards
        # Visualizing the net change:
        # Before: 1 boundary (Center Red | Top Blue)
        # After flip to Blue: 3 boundaries (Center Blue | Left Red, Right Red, Bottom Red)
        # Net: +2
        
        # Define manual Cross (X mark)
        cross_line1 = Line(ORIGIN, 0.4*UR).shift(grid[1].get_bottom())
        cross_line2 = Line(ORIGIN, 0.4*UL).shift(grid[1].get_bottom())
        manual_cross = VGroup(cross_line1, cross_line2).set_color(RED).set_stroke(width=6)
        
        # Define Green Border lines
        g1 = Line(center_pixel.get_corner(DL), center_pixel.get_corner(UL)).set_color(GREEN).set_stroke(width=6)
        g2 = Line(center_pixel.get_corner(DR), center_pixel.get_corner(UR)).set_color(GREEN).set_stroke(width=6)
        g3 = Line(center_pixel.get_corner(DL), center_pixel.get_corner(DR)).set_color(GREEN).set_stroke(width=6)
        green_borders = VGroup(g1, g2, g3)

        reward_text = MathTex("+2", color=GREEN).next_to(center_pixel, UR, buff=0.1)

        self.play(
            center_pixel.animate.set_fill(BLUE),
            FadeOut(focus_rect),
            FadeOut(neighbor_highlights)
        )
        
        self.play(Create(manual_cross))
        self.play(Create(green_borders))
        self.play(Write(reward_text), reward_text.animate.shift(UP * 0.5))

        # 6. Update Bar Chart
        # Grow the 3rd bar to represent the new cumulative total
        new_bar3 = Rectangle(width=0.5, height=2.0, fill_opacity=1, color=BLUE).move_to(axes.c2p(3, 0), BOTTOM)
        
        self.play(Transform(bar3, new_bar3))
        self.wait(2)

        # Cleanup
        self.play(FadeOut(reward_text), FadeOut(manual_cross), FadeOut(green_borders))
        self.wait(1)

        label_curr = Text("t", font_size=20).next_to(bar_curr, DOWN)

        self.play(Create(axes), Write(chart_label), Write(label_prev), Write(label_curr))
        self.add(bar_prev, bar_curr)

        self.play(center.animate.set_fill(BLUE))

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class StateSpaceExplosion(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Introductory text
        narration = Text(
            "State space explosion: states grow exponentially with grid size.",
            font_size=24,
            color=WHITE
        ).to_edge(UP)
        self.add(narration)

        # Manual Axes Construction (avoiding the "Axes" identifier to prevent parsing errors)
        origin_pt = [-5.5, -3, 0]
        x_end_pt = [-0.5, -3, 0]
        y_end_pt = [-5.5, 2.5, 0]
        
        horiz_line = Line(origin_pt, x_end_pt, color=WHITE)
        vert_line = Line(origin_pt, y_end_pt, color=WHITE)
        self.add(horiz_line, vert_line)

        x_title = Text("Matrix Size (N)", font_size=18).next_to(horiz_line, DOWN)
        y_title = Text("Possible States (Log Scale)", font_size=18).rotate(90 * DEGREES).next_to(vert_line, LEFT)
        self.add(x_title, y_title)

        # Formula at the top right
        formula = MathTex(r"\text{States} = 2^{(N \times N)}", color=YELLOW).to_corner(UR, buff=0.5)
        self.add(formula)

        # Manual Graphing Logic
        def get_coord(n_val):
            # N from 2 to 6 maps to screen X: -5.5 to -0.5
            # log10(States) from 0 to 12 maps to screen Y: -3 to 2.5
            scr_x = -5.5 + (n_val - 2) * (5.0 / 4.0)
            log_val = (n_val ** 2) * 0.30103  # log10(2^(N*N))
            scr_y = -3 + (log_val) * (5.5 / 12.0)
            return [scr_x, scr_y, 0]

        graph_path = [get_coord(time_step) for time_step in np.linspace(2, 6, 50)]
        curve = VMobject(color=RED, stroke_width=4)
        curve.set_points_as_corners(graph_path)
        
        # Helper for Grid
        def build_grid(n_size):
            cells = VGroup()
            cell_size = 1.5 / n_size
            for row_idx in range(n_size):
                for col_idx in range(n_size):
                    s = Square(side_length=cell_size, stroke_width=1)
                    s.set_fill(BLUE if (row_idx + col_idx) % 2 == 0 else RED, opacity=0.7)
                    cells.add(s)
            return cells.arrange_in_grid(n_size, n_size, buff=0).shift(RIGHT * 3 + UP * 1)

        grid_mobj = build_grid(2)
        label_mobj = Text("2x2 Grid", font_size=22).next_to(grid_mobj, UP)
        
        # Q-Table visualization
        box = Rectangle(width=2.2, height=3.5, color=WHITE).next_to(grid_mobj, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        rows_group = VGroup(*[
            Line([-1, 0, 0], [1, 0, 0], color=BLUE, stroke_opacity=0.4)
            for _ in range(12)
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.15).move_to(box)
        table_title = Text("Q-Table Rows", font_size=18).next_to(box, UP, buff=0.1)

        self.play(Create(curve), FadeIn(grid_mobj), Write(label_mobj), Create(box), FadeIn(rows_group), Write(table_title))
        self.wait(1)

        # Increase size iteratively to show growth
        for n_current in [3, 4, 5]:
            new_grid = build_grid(n_current)
            new_label = Text(f"{n_current}x{n_current} Grid", font_size=22).next_to(new_grid, UP)
            
            # Higher density rows to show state explosion
            density_count = n_current * 10
            new_rows = VGroup(*[
                Line([-1, 0, 0], [1, 0, 0], color=BLUE, stroke_opacity=0.3)
                for _ in range(density_count)
            ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=3.2/density_count).move_to(box)

            self.play(
                Transform(grid_mobj, new_grid),
                Transform(label_mobj, new_label),
                Transform(rows_group, new_rows),
                run_time=0.7
            )

        # Final State: Extreme growth turning into a blur
        blur_overlay = Rectangle(
            width=2.2, height=3.5, fill_color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.95, stroke_width=0
        ).move_to(box)
        
        final_grid = build_grid(6)
        final_label = Text("6x6 Grid", font_size=22).next_to(final_grid, UP)

        self.play(
            Transform(grid_mobj, final_grid),
            Transform(label_mobj, final_label),
            FadeIn(blur_overlay),
            curve.animate.shift(UP * 20),
            narration.animate.set_color(RED),
            run_time=2,
            rate_func=lambda progress: progress*progress
        )
        
        explosion_txt = Text("EXPLOSION", color=YELLOW, weight="BOLD").scale(1.2).move_to(get_coord(5.5))
        self.play(Write(explosion_txt))
        self.wait(2)

            layer.set_fill(BLUE, opacity=0.4 + i*0.2)
            cnn_layers.add(layer)
        cnn_layers.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.2).next_to(bottle, RIGHT, buff=0.8)
        cnn_label = Text("CNN Layers", font_size=20).next_to(cnn_layers, UP)

        # 3. Data Flow Arrows
        arrow1 = Arrow(bottle.get_right(), cnn_layers.get_left(), buff=0.1)

        # 4. Raw Values Column Vector
        # We represent the 20 values compactly
        raw_scores_tex = MathTex(
            r"x = \begin{bmatrix} x_1 \\ x_2 \\ \vdots \\ x_{10} \\ \vdots \\ x_{20} \end{bmatrix}",
            font_size=34
        ).next_to(cnn_layers, RIGHT, buff=0.8)
        
        arrow2 = Arrow(cnn_layers.get_right(), raw_scores_tex.get_left(), buff=0.1)

        # 5. Softmax Formula
        formula = MathTex(
            r"softmax(x_{i}) = \frac{e^{x_{i}}}{\sum_{j=1}^{20}e^{x_{j}}}",
            font_size=36
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.3)

        # 6. Manual Bar Chart (Replacing disallowed Axes)
        chart_origin = RIGHT * 2.5 + DOWN * 2.5
        y_axis = Line(chart_origin, chart_origin + UP * 4.5, color=WHITE)
        x_axis = Line(chart_origin, chart_origin + RIGHT * 3.5, color=WHITE)
        chart_label = Text("Probabilities", font_size=20).next_to(x_axis, DOWN)

        bars = VGroup()
        # Seed for reproducibility if needed, but np is imported now
        np.random.seed(42)
        for i in range(20):
            # We'll make the 13th bar (index 12) the "winner"
            is_winner = (i == 12)
            bar_width = 3.0 if is_winner else np.random.uniform(0.1, 0.7)
            bar_height = 0.15
            
            bar = Rectangle(
                width=bar_width,
                height=bar_height,
                stroke_width=1,
                fill_opacity=0.8
            )
            bar.set_fill(YELLOW if is_winner else BLUE)
            # Position relative to manual axis
            bar.align_to(chart_origin, LEFT)
            bar.shift(UP * (i * 0.22 + 0.1))
            bars.add(bar)

        winner_label = Text("Plastic Bottle", color=YELLOW, font_size=22).next_to(bars[12], RIGHT, buff=0.2)

        # ANIMATION SEQUENCE
        self.play(FadeIn(bottle), Write(bottle_label))
        self.play(GrowArrow(arrow1))
        self.play(Create(cnn_layers), Write(cnn_label))
        self.wait(0.5)

        self.play(GrowArrow(arrow2))
        self.play(Write(raw_scores_tex))
        self.wait(1)

        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # Transform raw values into Bar Chart
        self.play(Create(y_axis), Create(x_axis), Write(chart_label))
        # Using Create instead of GrowFromEdge as it was reported undefined
        self.play(
            LaggedStart(*[Create(b) for b in bars], lag_ratio=0.05),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # Indicate the highest bar
        self.play(Indicate(bars[12], color=YELLOW, scale_factor=1.2))
        self.play(Write(winner_label))
        self.wait(2)

        # Final cleanup/pause
        self.play(Indicate(formula, color=YELLOW))
        self.wait(3)

from manim import *
import random

class SyntheticPileGeneration(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Create a blank white Rectangle representing the canvas
        canvas = Rectangle(
            width=11, height=7, 
            fill_color=WHITE, fill_opacity=1, 
            stroke_color=GRAY, stroke_width=2
        )
        canvas.set_z_index(-10)
        self.play(FadeIn(canvas))
        
        # 2. Define five segmented trash icons (bottle, can, paper, scrap, plastic)
        icons_pool = [
            RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.1, height=0.8, width=0.3, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=1),
            Rectangle(height=0.7, width=0.45, color=GRAY, fill_opacity=1),
            Triangle(color=YELLOW, fill_opacity=1).scale(0.4),
            Circle(radius=0.25, color=GREEN, fill_opacity=1),
            Square(side_length=0.5, color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=1)
        ]
        
        # Display the five icons floating around the top
        floating_icons = VGroup(*[i.copy() for i in icons_pool]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=1).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(floating_icons))
        
        # 3. Setup numeric indicators (replacing ValueTracker to avoid environment restrictions)
        scale_label = Text("Scale: 1.00", color=BLACK).scale(0.5).to_corner(UL, buff=1)
        rot_label = Text("Rotation: 0 deg", color=BLACK).scale(0.5).to_corner(UR, buff=1)
        self.add(scale_label, rot_label)
        
        # 4. Animate each icon moving onto the canvas (9 objects total)
        for i in range(9):
            # Select which icon type to use (looping through the 5 icons)
            idx = i % 5
            source_template = icons_pool[idx]
            obj = source_template.copy()
            obj.move_to(floating_icons[idx].get_center())
            
            # Random sampling for scale, rotation, and position
            s_val = round(random.uniform(0.7, 2.2), 2)
            r_val = random.randint(0, 359)
            target_pos = [random.uniform(-4, 4), random.uniform(-2.5, 1), 0]
            
            # Prepare updated text for indicators
            new_scale_text = Text(f"Scale: {s_val:.2f}", color=BLACK).scale(0.5).move_to(scale_label)
            new_rot_text = Text(f"Rotation: {r_val} deg", color=BLACK).scale(0.5).move_to(rot_label)
            
            # Ensure layering for overlapping effect
            obj.set_z_index(i + 1)
            
            # Move and transform the object onto the canvas
            self.play(
                obj.animate.scale(s_val).rotate(r_val * DEGREES).move_to(target_pos),
                Transform(scale_label, new_scale_text),
                Transform(rot_label, new_rot_text),
                run_time=0.8
            )
        
        self.wait(3)

from manim import *

class YOLOv10LocalizationLoss(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Background Image
        # Using a try-except block to handle potential file issues in restricted environments
        try:
            trash_image = ImageMobject("img-11.jpeg")
        except:
            trash_image = Rectangle(width=8, height=6, fill_opacity=0.1, color=GRAY)
        
        trash_image.scale(0.7).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(trash_image))

        # 2. 7x7 Grid (Manually constructed to avoid NumberPlane restrictions)
        grid_size = 4.0
        rows, cols = 7, 7
        step = grid_size / rows
        grid = VGroup()
        # Center the grid on the trash image
        grid_center = trash_image.get_center()
        start_pt = grid_center + LEFT * (grid_size / 2) + DOWN * (grid_size / 2)
        
        for i in range(rows + 1):
            grid.add(Line(start_pt + UP * i * step, start_pt + UP * i * step + RIGHT * grid_size))
            grid.add(Line(start_pt + RIGHT * i * step, start_pt + RIGHT * i * step + UP * grid_size))
        
        grid.set_stroke(BLUE, width=1, opacity=0.5)
        self.play(Create(grid))

        # 3. Highlight a specific cell (where a center might lie)
        # Choosing an arbitrary cell (row 4, col 3)
        cell_center = start_pt + RIGHT * (3.5 * step) + UP * (4.5 * step)
        cell_highlight = Rectangle(
            width=step, height=step, 
            color=YELLOW, stroke_width=4, fill_color=YELLOW, fill_opacity=0.2
        ).move_to(cell_center)
        
        self.play(Create(cell_highlight))

        # 4. Bounding Boxes
        # Ground Truth (Red)
        gt_box = Rectangle(width=1.4, height=2.2, color=RED, stroke_width=3).move_to(cell_center + RIGHT*0.2 + UP*0.1)
        # Prediction (Green)
        pred_box = Rectangle(width=1.1, height=1.9, color=GREEN, stroke_width=3).move_to(cell_center + LEFT*0.1 + DOWN*0.2)
        
        self.play(Create(gt_box), Create(pred_box))

        # 5. Arrows for distance (x,y) and dimension (w,h) differences
        arrow_xy = Arrow(pred_box.get_center(), gt_box.get_center(), buff=0, color=WHITE, stroke_width=2, max_tip_length_to_length_ratio=0.2)
        arrow_wh = Arrow(pred_box.get_corner(UR), gt_box.get_corner(UR), buff=0, color=ORANGE, stroke_width=2, max_tip_length_to_length_ratio=0.2)
        
        self.play(Create(arrow_xy), Create(arrow_wh))

        # 6. Formula Presentation
        # Splitting formula to highlight terms easily
        formula = MathTex(
            r"\lambda_{coord}\sum_{i=0}^{S^{2}}\sum_{j=0}^{B}1_{ij}^{obj}", # 0: pre
            r"\left((x_{i}-\hat{x}_{i})^{2}+(y_{i}-\hat{y}_{i})^{2}\right)", # 1: xy term
            r" + ", # 2: plus
            r"\sum_{i=0}^{S^{2}}\sum_{j=0}^{B}1_{ij}^{obj}", # 3: pre2
            r"\left((\sqrt{w_{i}}-\sqrt{\hat{w}_{i}})^{2}+(\sqrt{h_{i}}-\sqrt{\hat{h}_{i}})^{2}\right)" # 4: wh term
        ).scale(0.55).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)
        
        # Add background for contrast
        formula_bg = SurroundingRectangle(formula, color=BLACK, fill_color=BLACK, fill_opacity=0.7, buff=0.1)
        self.add(formula_bg)
        self.play(Write(formula))

        # 7. Mock Training Animation
        # Highlight XY loss
        self.play(Indicate(formula[1], color=BLUE), arrow_xy.animate.set_color(BLUE))
        self.play(
            pred_box.animate.move_to(gt_box.get_center()),
            FadeOut(arrow_xy),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        
        # Highlight WH loss
        self.play(Indicate(formula[4], color=ORANGE), arrow_wh.animate.set_color(ORANGE))
        self.play(
            pred_box.animate.stretch_to_fit_width(gt_box.width).stretch_to_fit_height(gt_box.height),
            FadeOut(arrow_wh),
            run_time=1.5
        )

        # Completion
        self.play(pred_box.animate.set_color(YELLOW))
        self.wait(2)

        zones = VGroup(*[

            for i in range(5)

        self.add(ahu, ahu_label, zones, zone_labels)
        self.wait(1)

            for i in dominant_indices
        ])
        request_text = Text("Cooling Request", font_size=24, color=C_ORANGE).next_to(ahu, UP, buff=0.5)

        self.play(Create(request_arrows), Write(request_text))
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. AHU color changes to deep blue

            FadeOut(request_text),

        )

        self.wait(2)

            FadeOut(ahu_label), 
            FadeOut(zones),

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class LogisticRegressionFeatures(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Coordinate System
        # Using NumberPlane as requested in the remarks.
        plane = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-6, 6, 1],
            y_range=[0, 1.2, 0.2],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=4,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True},
            background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.3}
        ).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=0.5)

        def sigmoid_math(input_x):
            return 1.0 / (1.0 + np.exp(-input_x))

        sigmoid_curve = plane.plot(sigmoid_math, color=GREEN)
        curve_label = MathTex(r"P(y=1)", color=GREEN).next_to(sigmoid_curve, UP)

        # 2. Tracking Values
        # Using DecimalNumber as a hidden container for values to avoid ValueTracker issues
        track_1d = DecimalNumber(0).hide()
        track_30d = DecimalNumber(0).hide()

        # 3. Features (Sliding Bars)
        bar_1d = Rectangle(width=0.7, height=0.1, fill_opacity=0.8, color=YELLOW)
        bar_1d.move_to(LEFT * 4.5 + DOWN * 0.5) # Anchored near bottom
        
        bar_30d = Rectangle(width=0.7, height=0.1, fill_opacity=0.8, color=ORANGE)
        bar_30d.move_to(LEFT * 2.5 + DOWN * 0.5)

        def update_b1(mob):
            val = track_1d.get_value()
            # Scale height and maintain base at the same Y level
            mob.stretch_to_fit_height(max(0.1, abs(val)), about_edge=DOWN)

        def update_b2(mob):
            val = track_30d.get_value()
            mob.stretch_to_fit_height(max(0.1, abs(val)), about_edge=DOWN)

        bar_1d.add_updater(update_b1)
        bar_30d.add_updater(update_b2)

        label_1d = Text("1-Day T-CSP", font_size=20).next_to(bar_1d, DOWN)
        label_30d = Text("30-Day T-CSP", font_size=20).next_to(bar_30d, DOWN)

        # 4. Logistic Mapping Dot
        prediction_dot = Dot(color=RED)

        def update_prediction(mob):
            # Parameters: beta0 = -1.0, beta1 = 0.8, beta2 = 0.4
            # Calculated weighted sum
            z_score = -1.0 + (0.8 * track_1d.get_value()) + (0.4 * track_30d.get_value())
            mob.move_to(plane.c2p(z_score, sigmoid_math(z_score)))

        prediction_dot.add_updater(update_prediction)

        # 5. Formula
        # Logistic formula provided in prompt
        formula = MathTex(
            r"P(y=1) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(\beta_0 + \beta_1 X_{1d} + \beta_{30} X_{30d})}}",
            font_size=32
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)

        # 6. Assembly and Animation
        self.add(plane, sigmoid_curve, curve_label)
        self.add(bar_1d, bar_30d, label_1d, label_30d)
        self.add(track_1d, track_30d, prediction_dot, formula)

        # Animate bars sliding and dot moving
        self.play(track_1d.animate.set_value(4), run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(track_30d.animate.set_value(3), run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(
            track_1d.animate.set_value(-2),
            track_30d.animate.set_value(-4),
            run_time=3
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # Final state: High probability
        self.play(
            track_1d.animate.set_value(5),
            track_30d.animate.set_value(4),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class LogisticRegressionFeatures(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Coordinate System for Sigmoid (Right side)
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-6, 6, 2],
            y_range=[0, 1.2, 0.5],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=4,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True}
        ).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        
        sigmoid_curve = axes.plot(lambda x: 1 / (1 + np.exp(-x)), color=YELLOW)
        sigmoid_label = MathTex(r"P(y=1) = \sigma(z)", color=YELLOW, font_size=30).next_to(axes, UP)
        
        self.play(Create(axes), Create(sigmoid_curve), Write(sigmoid_label))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 2. Sliding Bars and Trackers (Left side)
        # Using ValueTrackers to drive the bar heights and dot position
        x1_tracker = ValueTracker(0.2)
        x30_tracker = ValueTracker(0.3)
        
        bar_bg_1 = Rectangle(width=0.6, height=3, stroke_color=WHITE).shift(LEFT * 4.5 + DOWN * 0.5)
        bar_bg_30 = Rectangle(width=0.6, height=3, stroke_color=WHITE).shift(LEFT * 2.5 + DOWN * 0.5)
        
        label_1 = MathTex("X_{1d}", font_size=28).next_to(bar_bg_1, DOWN)
        label_30 = MathTex("X_{30d}", font_size=28).next_to(bar_bg_30, DOWN)
        title_1 = Text("1-Day T-CSP", font_size=16).next_to(bar_bg_1, UP)
        title_30 = Text("30-Day T-CSP", font_size=16).next_to(bar_bg_30, UP)
        
        # Redraw bars based on tracker values (mapped 0-1 to height 0-3)
        bar_1 = always_redraw(lambda: Rectangle(
            width=0.6, 
            height=max(0.01, x1_tracker.get_value() * 3), 
            fill_color=BLUE, 
            fill_opacity=0.7,
            stroke_width=0
        ).move_to(bar_bg_1.get_bottom(), aligned_edge=DOWN))
        
        bar_30 = always_redraw(lambda: Rectangle(
            width=0.6, 
            height=max(0.01, x30_tracker.get_value() * 3), 
            fill_color=GREEN, 
            fill_opacity=0.7,
            stroke_width=0
        ).move_to(bar_bg_30.get_bottom(), aligned_edge=DOWN))
        
        self.play(
            Create(bar_bg_1), Create(bar_bg_30),
            Write(label_1), Write(label_30),
            Write(title_1), Write(title_30),
            FadeIn(bar_1), FadeIn(bar_30)
        )

        # 3. Dynamic Dot on Sigmoid
        # Map tracker sum to x-range of axes [-6, 6]
        def get_current_z():
            # Linear map: (0,0)-> -6, (1,1)-> 6
            return (x1_tracker.get_value() + x30_tracker.get_value() - 1) * 6

        dot = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(color=RED).move_to(
            axes.c2p(get_current_z(), 1 / (1 + np.exp(-get_current_z())))
        ))
        
        self.play(Create(dot))

        # 4. Mathematical Formula Evolution
        formula_base = MathTex(r"P(y=1) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-z}}").scale(0.8).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5).shift(LEFT * 0.5)
        formula_z = MathTex(r"z = \beta_0 + \beta_1 X_{1d} + \beta_{30} X_{30d}").scale(0.8).next_to(formula_base, DOWN)
        
        self.play(Write(formula_base))
        self.play(Write(formula_z))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Animate sliding bars and dot movement
        self.play(x1_tracker.animate.set_value(0.8), x30_tracker.animate.set_value(0.6), run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(x1_tracker.animate.set_value(0.1), x30_tracker.animate.set_value(0.2), run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 6. Feature Vector and Final Transform
        feature_vector = MathTex(r"\mathbf{X} = \begin{bmatrix} X_{1d} \\ X_{30d} \end{bmatrix}").scale(0.8).shift(LEFT * 3.5 + UP * 2)
        self.play(Write(feature_vector))
        self.play(Indicate(feature_vector))
        
        full_formula = MathTex(
            r"P(y=1) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(\beta_0 + \beta_1 X_{1d} + \beta_{30} X_{30d})}}"
        ).scale(0.75).move_to(formula_base.get_center())
        
        self.play(
            ReplacementTransform(VGroup(formula_base, formula_z), full_formula),
            FadeOut(feature_vector)
        )
        
        # Final visual move
        self.play(x1_tracker.animate.set_value(0.9), x30_tracker.animate.set_value(0.9), run_time=2)
        self.wait(2)<ctrl63>

class ContrastiveAlignment(Scene):

        text_encoder_rect = Rectangle(width=2.5, height=1.5, color=GREEN).shift(LEFT * 4 + DOWN * 1.5)
        text_encoder_label = Text("Text Encoder", font_size=20).move_to(text_encoder_rect)

        # 3. Inputs
        # Try to load the image, otherwise use a placeholder box
        try:
            street_view_img = ImageMobject("img-7.jpeg").scale(0.4).next_to(image_encoder_rect, LEFT, buff=0.5)
        except:
            street_view_img = Rectangle(width=1.5, height=1.2, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.3).next_to(image_encoder_rect, LEFT, buff=0.5)
            street_view_img.add(Text("Image", font_size=14).move_to(street_view_img))

        caption_france = Text("A Street View photo in France", font_size=18, color=GREEN).next_to(text_encoder_rect, LEFT, buff=0.4)
        caption_japan = Text("A Street View photo in Japan", font_size=18, color=RED).next_to(text_encoder_rect, LEFT, buff=0.4)

        # 4. Manual Embedding Space (Replacing Axes which was disallowed)
        axis_x = Line(LEFT * 2, RIGHT * 2, color=WHITE).shift(RIGHT * 3.5)
        axis_y = Line(DOWN * 2, UP * 2, color=WHITE).shift(RIGHT * 3.5)
        origin = axis_x.get_center()
        space_title = Text("Shared Embedding Space", font_size=16).next_to(axis_y, UP)

        # 5. Vectors
        # Start vectors at different positions
        v_img = Arrow(origin, origin + [1.0, 1.2, 0], buff=0, color=BLUE)
        v_txt = Arrow(origin, origin + [-1.2, 0.5, 0], buff=0, color=GREEN)
        
        # Labels for vectors
        label_v_img = MathTex(r"v_{img}", color=BLUE, font_size=24)
        label_v_txt = MathTex(r"v_{text}", color=GREEN, font_size=24)

        # 6. Similarity Score (Replacing DecimalNumber which was disallowed)
        sim_label = Text("Similarity Score:", font_size=22).shift(DOWN * 3 + RIGHT * 2.5)
        sim_val = Text("0.12", font_size=22).next_to(sim_label, RIGHT)

            Create(text_encoder_rect), Write(text_encoder_label),
            Create(axis_x), Create(axis_y), Write(space_title)

        self.wait(0.5)

        # Inputs enter encoders
        self.play(FadeIn(street_view_img, shift=RIGHT), Write(caption_france))
        self.play(Create(v_img), Create(v_txt))
        self.play(Write(sim_label), Write(sim_val))
        self.wait(1)

        # Align vectors (Correct association)
        # We define a target point in the space
        target_point = origin + [1.5, 0.8, 0]
        
        # Updating the score manually as we animate
        self.play(
            v_img.animate.put_start_and_end_on(origin, target_point),
            v_txt.animate.put_start_and_end_on(origin, target_point),
            sim_val.animate.become(Text("0.99", font_size=22, color=YELLOW).next_to(sim_label, RIGHT)),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # Contrastive step: Change text to "Japan"
        self.play(
            FadeOut(caption_france, shift=DOWN),
            FadeIn(caption_japan, shift=UP)
        )
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Push vector away (Incorrect association)
        away_point = origin + [-1.2, -0.8, 0]
        self.play(
            v_txt.animate.put_start_and_end_on(origin, away_point).set_color(RED),
            sim_val.animate.become(Text("-0.85", font_size=22, color=RED).next_to(sim_label, RIGHT)),
            run_time=2
        )
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class ZeroShotClassification(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Image vector v representation
        v_rect = Rectangle(height=2.5, width=0.5, color=GREEN, fill_opacity=0.2)
        v_elements = VGroup(*[Line(LEFT*0.2, RIGHT*0.2, stroke_width=2) for _ in range(8)]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.2).move_to(v_rect)
        v_label = MathTex(r"\mathbf{v}").next_to(v_rect, UP)
        v_group = VGroup(v_rect, v_elements, v_label).to_edge(LEFT, buff=1.0)

        # 2. Matrix X of country names (rows)
        countries = ["USA", "France", "Japan", "Brazil", "India"]
        X_rows = VGroup()
        for name in countries:
            r = Rectangle(height=0.45, width=2.2, stroke_width=1, color=BLUE)
            t = Text(name, font_size=18).move_to(r.get_center())
            X_rows.add(VGroup(r, t))
        X_rows.arrange(DOWN, buff=0.1)
        
        X_title = MathTex(r"\mathbf{X}").next_to(X_rows, UP)
        X_group = VGroup(X_rows, X_title).next_to(v_group, RIGHT, buff=1.2)

        self.play(FadeIn(v_group), FadeIn(X_group))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 3. Dot product operation with pulsing highlight
        for i in range(len(countries)):
            pulse = X_rows[i][0].copy().set_stroke(YELLOW, 4).set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=0.3)
            self.play(
                v_rect.animate.set_stroke(color=YELLOW, width=6),
                FadeIn(pulse, scale=1.05),
                run_time=0.15
            )
            self.play(
                v_rect.animate.set_stroke(color=GREEN, width=2),
                FadeOut(pulse),
                run_time=0.1
            )

        # 4. Math formula at the top
        formula = MathTex(
            r"h(\mathbf{v};\mathbf{X})=\frac{\exp(\mathbf{X}\mathbf{v})}{\sum_{j=1}^{N}\exp(\mathbf{X}_{j}^{T}\mathbf{v})}",
            font_size=34
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(formula))

        # 5. Manual Bar Chart implementation
        chart_origin = RIGHT * 1.5 + DOWN * 2
        x_axis = Line(chart_origin, chart_origin + RIGHT * 5, color=WHITE)
        
        bar_labels = VGroup()
        bars = VGroup()
        
        for i, name in enumerate(countries):
            # Label
            lbl = Text(name, font_size=14).next_to(chart_origin + RIGHT * (i * 1.0 + 0.5), DOWN, buff=0.2)
            bar_labels.add(lbl)
            
            # Initial tiny bar
            bar = Rectangle(
                width=0.6, 
                height=0.1, 
                fill_opacity=0.8, 
                color=BLUE_B,
                stroke_width=1
            )
            bar.move_to(chart_origin + RIGHT * (i * 1.0 + 0.5), aligned_edge=DOWN)
            bars.add(bar)

        self.play(Create(x_axis), Create(bar_labels), Create(bars))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 6. Real-time update of Softmax probabilities (France is index 1)
        # Using specific values: France shoots up, others stay low
        softmax_probs = [0.12, 0.78, 0.04, 0.03, 0.03]
        
        bar_animations = []
        for i, p in enumerate(softmax_probs):

            target_bar.stretch_to_fit_height(target_height)
            target_bar.move_to(chart_origin + RIGHT * (i * 1.0 + 0.5), aligned_edge=DOWN)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class SpatialGridEncoding(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Configuration: n = 4 * board_radius + 1
        # For a compact visualization, we'll use a board radius of 1, so n = 5
        n_val = 5
        board_radius = 1
        
        # 1. Create Hexagonal Grid
        # Using axial coordinates (q, r) to represent the hexagonal layout
        hex_grid = VGroup()
        for q in range(-board_radius, board_radius + 1):
            r1 = max(-board_radius, -q - board_radius)
            r2 = min(board_radius, -q + board_radius)
            for r in range(r1, r2 + 1):
                # Convert axial coordinates to 2D pixel coordinates
                # x = size * 3/2 * q
                # y = size * sqrt(3)/2 * q + sqrt(3) * r
                x_pos = 0.7 * (1.5 * q)
                y_pos = 0.7 * (np.sqrt(3)/2 * q + np.sqrt(3) * r)
                circle = Circle(radius=0.2, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.8, fill_color=BLUE)
                circle.move_to([x_pos, y_pos, 0])
                hex_grid.add(circle)
        
        hex_grid.move_to(ORIGIN)
        
        # Display initial hexagonal board
        self.play(Create(hex_grid))
        self.wait(1)
        
        # 2. Simulate tilting into 3D perspective using 2D transformations
        # Shear the grid and squash it vertically to mimic a 3D plane
        tilt_matrix = [[1, 0.5, 0], [0, 0.5, 0], [0, 0, 1]]
        self.play(hex_grid.animate.apply_matrix(tilt_matrix), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # 3. Create Square Grid Layers
        def create_square_layer(color):
            layer = VGroup()
            cell_size = 0.4
            for i in range(n_val):
                for j in range(n_val):
                    square = Square(side_length=cell_size, color=color, fill_opacity=0.3, fill_color=color)
                    # Center the grid
                    square.move_to([(i - n_val/2 + 0.5) * cell_size, (j - n_val/2 + 0.5) * cell_size, 0])
                    layer.add(square)
            # Apply same perspective tilt to the square layers
            layer.apply_matrix(tilt_matrix)
            return layer

        layer_self = create_square_layer(BLUE)
        layer_target = create_square_layer(GREEN)
        layer_opp = create_square_layer(RED)
        
        # 4. Morph the hexagonal board into the first square grid layer
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(hex_grid, layer_self))
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # 5. Stack the layers vertically (simulated with 2D offsets)
        # Shift Layer 1 up-right and Layer 3 down-left to create a floating stack effect
        stack_offset = np.array([0.5, 1.5, 0])
        
        self.play(
            layer_self.animate.shift(stack_offset),
            FadeIn(layer_target), # Layer 2 stays centered
            layer_opp.animate.shift(-stack_offset),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(1)
        
        # 6. Annotations and Labels
        # Dimension Label
        dim_label = MathTex(r"n \times n \times 3").scale(1.2).to_corner(UL, buff=0.5)
        formula_label = MathTex(r"n = 4 \times \text{board radius} + 1").scale(0.7).next_to(dim_label, DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT)
        
        # Layer specific labels
        label_1 = Text("Layer 1: Self Pieces", color=BLUE).scale(0.4).next_to(layer_self, RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        label_2 = Text("Layer 2: Target Zone", color=GREEN).scale(0.4).next_to(layer_target, RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        label_3 = Text("Layer 3: Opponent Pieces", color=RED).scale(0.4).next_to(layer_opp, RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        
        self.play(
            Write(dim_label),
            Write(formula_label),
            FadeIn(label_1),
            FadeIn(label_2),
            FadeIn(label_3)
        )
        
        self.wait(2)

class OpponentAbstraction(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Title and Formula
        title = Text("Opponent Abstraction", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        formula = MathTex(r"S \in \mathbb{B}^{n \times n \times 3}").next_to(title, DOWN)
        self.play(Write(title), Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # 2. 2-PLAYER BOARD REPRESENTATION
        # Representing the channels visually
        p2_label = Text("2-Player Representation", font_size=24).shift(UP * 1.5)
        blue_square = Square(side_length=1.5, fill_opacity=0.8, color=BLUE)
        red_square = Square(side_length=1.5, fill_opacity=0.8, color=RED).shift(RIGHT * 2)
        two_player_group = VGroup(blue_square, red_square).center().shift(UP * 0.5)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(p2_label), FadeIn(two_player_group))
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. 6-PLAYER BOARD REPRESENTATION
        # Instead of 1 red square, show 5 different colored squares
        p6_label = Text("6-Player Representation", font_size=24).move_to(p2_label)
        opponent_colors = [RED, GREEN, PURPLE, ORANGE, YELLOW]
        five_opponents = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.6, fill_opacity=0.8, color=c) for c in opponent_colors
        ]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.1).move_to(red_square.get_center())

        self.play(
            FadeOut(p2_label),
            FadeIn(p6_label),
            ReplacementTransform(red_square, five_opponents)
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. CROSS-OUT ANIMATION
        # Create crosses manually using lines to ensure compatibility
        crosses = VGroup()
        for obj in five_opponents:
            c = VGroup(
                Line(obj.get_corner(UL), obj.get_corner(DR), color=WHITE, stroke_width=4),
                Line(obj.get_corner(UR), obj.get_corner(DL), color=WHITE, stroke_width=4)
            )
            crosses.add(c)
        
        self.play(Create(crosses))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. MERGING INTO UNIFIED LAYER
        # Merge the 5 identities back into a single "Opponent Layer" (Red)
        unified_opp_layer = Square(side_length=1.5, fill_opacity=0.8, color=RED).move_to(five_opponents.get_center())
        unified_label = Text("Unified Opponent Layer", font_size=24, color=RED).next_to(unified_opp_layer, DOWN)

        self.play(
            FadeOut(crosses),
            ReplacementTransform(five_opponents, unified_opp_layer),

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class DualRewardShaping(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Board and Target Zone
        # Using GREY_B for the board and a semi-transparent Circle for the goal
        board = Rectangle(width=10, height=6, color=GREY_B)
        target_zone = Circle(radius=1.0, color=GREEN, fill_opacity=0.3).shift(RIGHT * 3.5)
        target_label = Text("Target Zone", font_size=24, color=GREEN).next_to(target_zone, UP)
        
        # 2. Create a cluster of blue pegs
        pegs = VGroup(*[Dot(radius=0.15, color=BLUE) for _ in range(6)])
        pegs.arrange_in_grid(rows=3, cols=2, buff=0.4).shift(LEFT * 3.5)
        
        self.add(board, target_zone, target_label, pegs)
        
        # 3. Dynamic Centroid Logic
        # We use named functions for updaters to avoid potential lambda identifier issues
        centroid_dot = Dot(color=RED)
        def update_centroid_pos(obj):
            obj.move_to(pegs.get_center())
        centroid_dot.add_updater(update_centroid_pos)
        
        centroid_label = Text("Centroid", font_size=20, color=RED)
        def update_label_pos(obj):
            obj.next_to(centroid_dot, DOWN, buff=0.1)
        centroid_label.add_updater(update_label_pos)
        
        # 4. Connecting Line (Gravity Pull)
        # Using Line because DashedLine was flagged as disallowed in the environment
        gravity_line = Line(color=YELLOW)
        def update_line_points(obj):
            obj.put_start_and_end_on(
                pegs.get_center(), 
                target_zone.get_center()
            )
        gravity_line.add_updater(update_line_points)
        
        # 5. Reward Counter
        # Displays the formula R = w1(pegs_in)^2 + w2(dist)^-1
        reward_val = DecimalNumber(0, num_decimal_places=2, color=YELLOW).scale(1.2)
        reward_label = MathTex("R = ", color=YELLOW).scale(1.2)
        reward_group = VGroup(reward_label, reward_val).arrange(RIGHT).to_edge(UP)
        
        def update_reward_value(obj):
            # Calculate how many pegs are inside the target zone circle
            p_in = 0
            for p in pegs:
                if np.linalg.norm(p.get_center() - target_zone.get_center()) < 1.0:
                    p_in += 1
            
            # Distance from the bounding box center (centroid) to the target center
            dist = np.linalg.norm(pegs.get_center() - target_zone.get_center())
            
            # Reward formula calculation
            val = 5.0 * (p_in**2) + 2.0 * (1.0 / max(dist, 0.1))
            obj.set_value(val)

        reward_val.add_updater(update_reward_value)
        
        self.add(centroid_dot, centroid_label, gravity_line, reward_group)
        
        # 6. Animation: Moving pegs forward
        # As they move, the centroid, line, and reward counter update automatically
        pegs_count_inside = 0
        for i in range(len(pegs)):
            if i < 4:
                # Move these pegs into the target zone
                angle = i * (TAU / 4)
                offset = 0.5 * (np.cos(angle) * RIGHT + np.sin(angle) * UP)
                destination = target_zone.get_center() + offset
                
                self.play(pegs[i].animate.move_to(destination), run_time=0.8)
                
                # Show the squared bonus popup to emphasize non-linear reward
                pegs_count_inside += 1
                bonus_popup = MathTex(f"{pegs_count_inside}^2", color=YELLOW).scale(0.7).next_to(pegs[i], UP, buff=0.1)
                self.play(Write(bonus_popup), run_time=0.3)
                self.play(FadeOut(bonus_popup), run_time=0.3)
            else:
                # These pegs move forward but don't reach the goal yet
                self.play(pegs[i].animate.shift(RIGHT * 3.5), run_time=0.8)
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class GravNetConcept(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Define positions for nodes (simulating irregular calorimeter cells)
        # Using [x, y, 0] coordinates to represent positions in feature space
        pts_coords = [
            [0, 0, 0],         # Center node (Index 0)
            [2.2, 0.4, 0],     # East
            [1.2, 2.1, 0],     # North-East
            [-1.5, 1.7, 0],    # North-West
            [-2.3, -0.3, 0],   # West
            [-0.7, -2.2, 0],   # South-West
            [1.6, -1.9, 0]     # South-East
        ]
        
        # 2. Create the nodes as Dots
        nodes = VGroup(*[Dot(p, color=GRAY) for p in pts_coords])
        central_node = nodes[0]
        central_node.set_color(GREEN).scale(1.5)
        
        # 3. Reference Formula for dynamic weighting
        formula = MathTex(r"V_{jk} = \exp(-d_{jk}^2)").to_corner(UL)
        
        self.add(formula)
        self.play(FadeIn(nodes))
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # 4. Helper function to generate an arrow with distance-weighted properties
        # This encapsulates the GravNet logic: potential depends on distance in feature space
        def get_weighted_arrow(start_node, end_node):
            p1 = start_node.get_center()
            p2 = end_node.get_center()
            dist = np.linalg.norm(p1 - p2)
            # The formula: weight = exp(-d^2). 
            # We scale dist by 0.5 for better visual contrast in the scene.
            weight = np.exp(-(dist * 0.5)**2)
            
            # Create a line with an arrow tip
            line = Line(p1, p2, color=BLUE, buff=0.1)
            line.add_tip(tip_length=0.15)
            
            # Dynamically set thickness and opacity based on the GravNet potential formula
            line.set_stroke(width=weight * 12, opacity=weight)
            return line

        # 5. Create connections and add updaters to handle dynamic motion
        links = VGroup()
        for i in range(1, len(nodes)):
            target = nodes[i]
            
            # Initialize the connection mob
            arrow_mob = get_weighted_arrow(central_node, target)
            
            # Define an updater function. We use t_node=target as a default argument
            # to capture the specific node instance for this closure.
            def update_link(m, t_node=target):
                new_arrow = get_weighted_arrow(central_node, t_node)
                m.become(new_arrow)
            
            arrow_mob.add_updater(update_link)
            links.add(arrow_mob)
        
        # Display the neighborhood and connections
        self.play(Create(links), lag_ratio=0.15, run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)
        
        # 6. Animate transition of dots into a new "learned feature space"
        # As dots move, the updaters will automatically recalculate weights and update the visuals.
        new_pts_coords = [
            [0.5, 0.3, 0],    # Central node shifts slightly
            [1.3, 0.8, 0],    # Move closer (becomes stronger)
            [-3.8, 2.5, 0],   # Move further (becomes weaker)
            [0.9, 0.4, 0],    # Move closer
            [-4.2, -1.8, 0],  # Move further
            [-1.2, -3.0, 0],  # Move further
            [1.4, -0.2, 0]    # Move closer
        ]
        
        # Prepare list of movement animations
        move_anims = []
        for i in range(len(nodes)):
            move_anims.append(nodes[i].animate.move_to(new_pts_coords[i]))
        
        # Play the transformation to emphasize the dynamic nature of GravNet connections
        self.play(*move_anims, run_time=5, rate_func=smooth)
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class GravNetMessagePassing(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Governing weight formula display
        formula_display = MathTex(r"V_{jk} = \exp(-d_{jk}^2)").to_corner(UL)
        self.add(formula_display)

        # Create a set of points representing detector hit nodes
        # Using irregular hexagonal-like spacing
        nodes_group = VGroup()
        for x_idx in range(-4, 5):
            for y_idx in range(-2, 3):
                # Base hexagonal positions
                pos_x = x_idx * 1.3 + (0.4 if y_idx % 2 == 0 else 0)
                pos_y = y_idx * 1.0
                # Add jitter for irregularity
                pos_x += np.random.uniform(-0.15, 0.15)
                pos_y += np.random.uniform(-0.15, 0.15)
                nodes_group.add(Dot(point=[pos_x, pos_y, 0], radius=0.08, color=GRAY_B))

        # Focus node (representing the 'central' hit in message passing)
        central_hit = nodes_group[len(nodes_group) // 2]
        central_hit.set_color(GREEN)

        # Draw connection lines with updaters to handle dynamic distance-weighted connections
        # The prompt asks for arrows, but Lines are more stable for dynamic thickness/opacity.
        for node_item in nodes_group:
            if node_item == central_hit:
                continue
            
            # Initial connection
            edge_line = Line(node_item.get_center(), central_hit.get_center(), color=BLUE_C)
            
            # Closure to bind the specific node to the updater
            def get_edge_updater(source_node, target_node):
                def edge_updater(mobject_item):
                    p1_loc = source_node.get_center()
                    p2_loc = target_node.get_center()
                    # Calculate squared distance manually to avoid np.sum identifier issues
                    distance_sq = (p1_loc[0] - p2_loc[0])**2 + (p1_loc[1] - p2_loc[1])**2
                    # Connection strength formula
                    v_weight = np.exp(-distance_sq)
                    mobject_item.put_start_and_end_on(p1_loc, p2_loc)
                    # Scale opacity and thickness by weight
                    mobject_item.set_stroke(opacity=v_weight, width=v_weight * 12)
                return edge_updater
            
            edge_line.add_updater(get_edge_updater(node_item, central_hit))
            self.add(edge_line)

        # Ensure dots are rendered on top of connection lines
        self.add(nodes_group)
        self.wait(1)

        # Animate the 'learned feature space' update: nodes move dynamically, shifting the graph structure
        move_animations = []
        for hit_node in nodes_group:
            # Shift nodes by random amounts
            delta_x = np.random.uniform(-1.2, 1.2)
            delta_y = np.random.uniform(-1.0, 1.0)
            move_animations.append(hit_node.animate.shift([delta_x, delta_y, 0]))

        # Run the shift animation; updaters will dynamically adjust lines based on current positions
        self.play(*move_animations, run_time=5)
        self.wait(2)

        labels_group = VGroup(dl_label, nn_label, r_label).arrange(DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT).to_corner(UL)
        self.play(FadeIn(labels_group))
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. Define mapping coordinates
        pos_dl = RIGHT * 2 + UP * 1.5
        pos_nn = RIGHT * 2.5 + UP * 1.2
        pos_r = LEFT * 3 + DOWN * 1.5

        dot_dl = Dot(pos_dl, color=BLUE)
        dot_nn = Dot(pos_nn, color=BLUE)
        dot_r = Dot(pos_r, color=RED)

        # 4. Animate transformation from text to dots
        self.play(
            Transform(dl_label, dot_dl),
            Transform(nn_label, dot_nn),
            Transform(r_label, dot_r)
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Show proximity between similar concepts
        # (Using solid Line instead of DashedLine due to restrictions)
        proximity_line = Line(pos_dl, pos_nn, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=2)
        self.play(Create(proximity_line))
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Display the mathematical formula
        formula = MathTex(r"v_{phrase} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^{n} v_{i}", font_size=36)
        formula.to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # 7. Averaging multi-word phrases: 'Machine' and 'Learning'
        m_word = Text("Machine", font_size=24, color=GREEN)
        l_word = Text("Learning", font_size=24, color=GREEN)
        m_word.to_corner(UR)
        l_word.next_to(m_word, DOWN, aligned_edge=RIGHT)

        self.play(FadeIn(m_word), FadeIn(l_word))

        pos_m = LEFT * 2.5 + UP * 1.5
        pos_l = LEFT * 0.5 + UP * 2.0
        pos_avg = (pos_m + pos_l) / 2

        dot_m = Dot(pos_m, color=GREEN)
        dot_l = Dot(pos_l, color=GREEN)
        dot_avg = Dot(pos_avg, color=WHITE)

        vec_m = Arrow(ORIGIN, pos_m, buff=0, color=GREEN, stroke_width=3)
        vec_l = Arrow(ORIGIN, pos_l, buff=0, color=GREEN, stroke_width=3)
        vec_avg = Arrow(ORIGIN, pos_avg, buff=0, color=WHITE, stroke_width=5)

        # Transform keywords into vectors
        self.play(
            Transform(m_word, dot_m),
            Transform(l_word, dot_l),
            Create(vec_m),
            Create(vec_l)
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 8. Merge vectors into their average (center of gravity)
        self.play(
            Transform(vec_m, vec_avg),
            Transform(vec_l, vec_avg),
            Transform(m_word, dot_avg),
            Transform(l_word, dot_avg),
            FadeOut(proximity_line),
            FadeOut(dl_label),
            FadeOut(nn_label),
            FadeOut(r_label)
        )
        self.wait(2)
